info.title: Generate reusable Spoon rule for Maven API breakage

parts.text: "Project @guice/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @guice/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/struts2-core-2.5.26-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-struts2-core-2.5.26/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @guice/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Examining code modifications**

I need to modify some code and I'm thinking there might be something missing in the repo. I should inspect the filesystem to see what's there. A todo list could help since it’s a multi-step process. I'll follow the commentary updates and use the right tools strictly for file operations. I can use glob, read, or grep through commentary for this purpose. Plus, I should check if I can do parallel reads to streamline things. Let's inspect the directories!
I’m locating the project, the Spoon template, and the dependency API docs first so I can derive the breaking change from the actual compile failures.
